name: Apple Foundation Model 3 Core (on-device, ~3B)
archDisplay: Dense on-device transformer, third generation of Apple's on-device model line
note: Third-generation on-device model announced at WWDC26 (2026-06-08), a quality step up from the 2025 on-device model; gains image-input understanding via the Foundation Models framework's new multimodal update.
verificationNotes: Confirmed via Apple's own machinelearning.apple.com research post, 9to5Mac's WWDC26 explainer, and MacRumors' June 9, 2026 coverage of the Platforms State of the Union. ctxK=32 is CARRIED FORWARD from the prior-generation on-device model (Apple did not publish a new context-window figure for this generation in the sources found) — flagged as an estimate, not a confirmed new number. No standard-benchmark (MMLU/GPQA-style) numeric scores were published for this generation — Apple reported only internal human-preference win-rates (e.g. 45.6% vs 23.3% preferred over the 2025 model on text tasks), which are not on the fixed benchmark-slug list, so no 'results' rows are included rather than mis-mapping them. This is a genuine gap: the corpus's Apple folder only has the original ~3B on-device model from June 2024.
